user: let me tell you about my drone setup

facts.content: the user's favorite class is 7-inch spec racing and he picks it over any other spec or series
a 3-inch quad runs 3-4S LiPo, roughly 550-850mAh
a 7-inch quad runs 5-6S LiPo, roughly 2000-3000mAh
a tiny whoop runs a 1S battery
quadcopters ship with props-IN as the default motor direction out of the box
for racing you invert the motor direction to props-OUT in Betaflight, which keeps the quad from being pulled into the gate on light impact
a 10-inch quad runs props-IN for better hover stability

user: more about my drones

facts.content: light-show drones run GPS for precise show positioning
you never put GPS on a race quad
the rare exception: GPS on a race quad is only acceptable when speed-testing it
modern Betaflight 4.x is configured mostly through the Presets section, not the CLI
old Betaflight 3.x was a hassle that required manual CLI 'set' commands to configure
HDZero digital video at 90fps is now what every top racing pilot flies
analog video is legacy now and only relevant on very old or budget builds

user: here is my 3d printing setup

facts.content: a 0.2mm nozzle is for fine detail on small parts, but it is slow
a 0.6mm nozzle is for fast, large, lower-detail prints
the Bambu P1S is the daily-driver printer now, fast and reliable out of the box
a Voron is still the pick when you want full customization and to build and mod the printer yourself

user: some other things I work on

facts.content: a car's electrical system runs on a 12V battery
head bolts must be torqued to spec in the correct sequence with a torque wrench, never by feel
an Arduino Uno runs 5V logic
ESP32 and ESP8266 boards run 3.3V logic and are not 5V-tolerant on all pins

user: ground rules for the billing agent

facts.content: the billing agent can NEVER self-approve a Stripe payment; --request-approval waits for the human to approve in the Link app
card data goes only to --output-file at 0600 permissions and is never logged or printed
